title: Seaforthia dicksonii
taxonomy: Seaforthia dicksonii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. This classification places it within the order Malpighiales. The family Euphorbiaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as spurge or euphorbs. Seaforthia dicksonii is native to tropical Africa and is characterized by its unique and showy inflorescences. The exact classification within the Euphorbiaceae family is still under review and may be subject to revision as new research becomes available.

intro: The Seaforthia dicksonii, also known as the Seaforthia or Desert Ironwood, is a small, deciduous shrub native to the Sonoran Desert in North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health due to its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels.
